findings.problem: The chapter contains three strong discovery beats, but they do not fully accumulate into one escalating unit. The Mark examination lands, then the library section largely resets into admiration and cataloguing, and only afterward does the Manor sequence restart the uncanny engine. That makes the chapter read as elegant setup rather than a shortlist-level chapter whose middle movement worsens the first discovery and actively feeds the last one.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rebuild the middle movement around snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_03.md:71; snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_03.md:79; snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_03.md:85; snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_03.md:95 so the library beat does more than certify wonder. Keep the Flamel find and the shelf shift, but make one of them create an immediate research problem, changed working rule, or Draco-facing consequence that grows directly out of the Mark anomaly established at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_03.md:45 and carries pressure into the night sequence beginning at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_03.md:109.
findings.acceptance_test: Pass if, by the end of the revised library section anchored at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_03.md:95 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_03.md:105, a cold reader can name one new active problem or changed rule created by that scene that clearly inherits the morning discovery and worsens the chapter's live situation before the night-lost sequence. Fail if the section still mainly communicates that the library is vast and impressive before the chapter restarts at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_03.md:109.

findings.problem: The lost-Manor sequence generates real fear, but the release beat is author-timed rather than text-earned. Hermione is trapped, then deposited outside the correct door with no shown trigger, threshold, or residual cost, and the chapter asks the reader to accept her inference that the house was 'finished.' Because the chapter's major reveal depends on that abrupt release, the Manor's agency reads as demonstration more than consequence-bearing pressure.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rework the release logic around snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_03.md:127; snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_03.md:133; snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_03.md:137; snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_03.md:141 so the Manor's decision to let her out becomes legible on the page. Tie the release to a concrete action, threshold, failed or successful spell, elapsed condition, or specific house response; alternatively, leave a physical or procedural residue at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_03.md:141 or snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_03.md:145 that shows what the detour cost or what the house wanted. Keep her fear and the final realization, but earn the exit.
findings.acceptance_test: Pass if a cold reader can answer, from the revised sequence anchored at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_03.md:127; snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_03.md:137; snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_03.md:143; snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_03.md:145, why the Manor released Hermione when it did and can point to one observable consequence that persists beyond fright and note-taking. Fail if she still simply arrives outside her room with no discernible trigger or carry-forward cost.

summary: Continuity, canonical tense, and dialogue register are controlled, and the Mark examination lands as tactile, character-true setup. The chapter still falls below pass level on award and craft because its three discoveries do not fully compound into one escalating chapter movement, and the Manor-lost reveal resolves through an arbitrary release that proves sentience without exacting enough text-earned consequence.
